advocate.definition: A person who publicly supports or recommends a particular cause or policy.

alleviate.definition: To make suffering, deficiency, or a problem less severe.

ambiguous.definition: Open to more than one interpretation; having a double meaning.

arbitrary.definition: Based on random choice or personal whim, rather than reason or a system.

contemplate.definition: To look thoughtfully for a long time or to consider deeply.

deduce.definition: To arrive at a fact or conclusion by reasoning.

diligent.definition: Showing care and conscientiousness in one's work or duties.

discrepancy.definition: A lack of compatibility or similarity between two or more facts.

emphasize.definition: To give special importance or prominence to something.

feasible.definition: Possible to do easily or conveniently.

fluctuate.definition: To rise and fall irregularly in number or amount.

hinder.definition: To create difficulties for someone or something, resulting in delay or obstruction.

inherent.definition: Existing in something as a permanent, essential, or characteristic attribute.

meticulous.definition: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise.

preliminary.definition: Denoting an action or event preceding or done in preparation for something fuller or more important.

scrutinize.definition: To examine or inspect closely and thoroughly.
